ogTitle: Maranup vs Marble Bar: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Maranup and Marble Bar.
heading: Maranup vs Marble Bar — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Maranup, Western Australia and Marble Bar, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Marble Bar has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Maranup (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Maranup or Marble Bar safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Maranup has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Marble Bar scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Maranup scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Marble Bar scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Maranup or Marble Bar?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Marble Bar has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
